claim.content: Numerical calculations for alternating-twist four-layer and five-layer graphene, using the same drive parameters $A=0.08\,a^{-1}$ and $\hbar\Omega=6\,\mathrm{eV}$ together with lattice-relaxation-informed effective interlayer tunneling ratios and magic-angle conditions, show that the central Floquet flat band remains gapped and topological at the first magic angle in both layer numbers. In the four-layer case the decomposition contains a twisted-bilayer-graphene subsystem and a nonmagic twisted-bilayer-graphene-like subsystem, and in the five-layer case it contains a twisted-bilayer-graphene subsystem together with an additional non-twisted-bilayer subsystem and a graphene subsystem. The corresponding phase diagrams contain additional gap closings at $\Gamma_{M}$ and $K_M$ arising from extra Dirac cones, but the lattice-relaxed first-magic-angle parameter points lie inside gapped topological regions. Floquet driving therefore produces topological flat bands in four- and five-layer alternating-twist multilayer graphene under realistic relaxation-modified parameters.
